summary: Frontend-only validation of the new Enrichment Engine tab in NEXUS SaaS. Logged in as admin@nexus.io/nexus123, verified nav-enrich appears with '5' tag, all 5 enrichment type cards render and switch the form fields correctly, ran live AI enrichment for all 5 types (business / person / osint / property / lead-composite) — every call returned valid JSON with the expected keys within timeout. Regression-navigated all 10 other tabs (overview, scrapers, leads, people, osint, ai, reports, billing, keys, admin) — every tab rendered without white-screen or pageerror. Logout returned to the login screen. 100% pass.
critical_code_review_comments: tabs.jsx is 599 lines and contains Overview, Osint, AIChat, ApiKeys, Reports, Admin, PeopleIntel, Billing, Enrichment — consider splitting each into its own file for maintainability (per >700-line rule it's still acceptable, but trending close).

In Enrichment.run(), for type='lead' the body always includes model + maybe business/osint; if both name and target are empty the request still fires — could add a small client-side guard to avoid an empty composite request and an AI hit. Minor UX nit only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Enrichment tab fully functional end-to-end with live AI (DeepSeek). All 5 endpoints respond with expected JSON keys (business: industry/employee_range/tech_stack/quality_score; person: profiles/ai_profile; osint: identity/accounts; property: estimated:true; lead: business+osint+score{grade}). AI latency observed ~3-15s per call — when re-testing, poll enrich-result for JSON content length>50 instead of fixed sleeps. nav-enrich data-testid='nav-enrich' shows label 'Enrichment' with '5' badge.
